target: 31. The Fourth Funeral ~ Interrogation
(1:30. Ushio's coffin went into the furnace.)
(One hour until we collect her ashes... It's time for everyone to have lunch.)
(This is the perfect chance to gather information. I'll try throwing two questions at the people I'm curious about.)
(First, do they know about the Shadow Sickness... And second, have they seen a woman with a large chest...?)
(Who should I talk to…)
Ask Totsumura
Ask Alain
Ask Karikiri
Ask Hishigata

Totsumura-san.
Have you heard of the Shadow Sickness?
Ahhhhh, I've heard of that~~~! It's a ghost story from Hitogashima, right! I was scared of it when I was little~~~~.
They'd say that bad kids who don't listen will get eaten by Shadows...
...Do you think Shadows actually exist?
Eh? No, no, no, it's just a superstition! There's no way something like that actually exists!!
Right, of course... Um, can I ask one more thing?
Huh?
Um... someone wearing glasses... How do I say this... did you see a woman with a large chest?
Ahhhhh!!!! I saw her, I saw her!! Or rather, I talked to her!!!!
She seemed kinda dangerous? She suddenly tried to open Ushio-chan's coffin and all~~.
But man, she was a beauty!! And her boobs! She said they were G-cups! G!!
A real G-cup!! Have you ever seen one!? It was insane! So hot!!
D-Did you get her contact info...?
Actually, about that!! I was gonna ask, but she ran away! I should've arrested her~~~~!!
*Sigh*... I see... Thank you very much...

Alain.
Have you heard of the Shadow Sickness?
Shadows…?
Ah... I feel like I might have heard something like that from Kotoko a long time ago...
(Kotoko... Ushio and Mio's mother...)
That's a so-called doppelgänger, right. It's a famous story in France too! There was a person named Emilie Sagée...
Did Ushio ever tell you about this?
Ushio??
No...? I haven't heard anything about Shadows from her...

I see…
Hey, I have one more question.
What?
Did you see someone wearing glasses, with black hair... like... a model with a really good figure?

Glasses… black hair… model…?
Ah, it's Hizuru!

…!
Do you know her?
Yeah... she came by here too. She used to come eat at our place a lot in the past. She was a regular.
Huh. Really?

Yeah. She left the island a while back,
so you probably don't remember her.
She left the island…?
Yeah, when Hizuru was in middle school. Well... a lot of things happened. She hadn't come back to the island at all since then.
(If he's dodging the question, it must be something hard to talk about. I shouldn't pry any further.)
So that means she's back visiting her hometown now. Did you contact her, Alain?
Me? No, no! I didn't even know her contact info. It seems she came to the island for work...
Work?
She said she's working as a writer in Tokyo.
I see... Thanks.
Karikiri-san. Do you know about the Shadow Sickness?
What do you mean do I know about it... The one who named it "Shadow Sickness" was my great-grandfather.
Eh!? Is that so!?

Yes… in the late Edo period.
Back then, everything was blamed on yokai…
I heard that up until my grandfather's generation, they even performed exorcisms at the shrine...
Huh... And how effective were they, by the way?
Effective!? Hmmmm, I wonder...
Have you ever done one, Karikiri-san?
I've never done one... I do normal exorcisms, though...
Ah! More importantly, Shinpei-kun. How is Tokyo?
Actually, I went there last year! You know that place near the sea, what was it called--
(Crap, this person talks forever once he gets started... I have to push the conversation forward, even if I have to force it!)
Um, can I ask one more thing? With glasses, black hair... and a large chest... Have you seen a woman like that?
Hmm... Could that be Hizuru-chan? Hizuru Minakata-chan!
Um... huh... I wonder if she's still around here?
(I don't see anyone matching that description, though...)
<color=#GF_TIPS_119>Hishigata-sensei</color>. I have something I want to ask you... Is now a good time?
Ah, it's you... What do you want to ask?
I know it's a bit weird to ask a doctor about this, but... Do you know about... the Shadow Sickness?
Ah... I know of it, yes. ...It's a fact that a certain kind of "mental illness"... occurred collectively on this island.
So there are records of it?
Yes, there are old medical records left at my clinic too.
It's called <color=#GF_TIPS_057>Capgras syndrome</color>... The patient believes that close people, like friends or family, have been replaced by identical imposters...
...!! By the way, Sensei, have you ever treated a case?
Me? ...Of course not! This is a story from the early Showa period. I have no experience treating such cases.

I see… Oh, one more thing.
Do you know a woman with glasses and black hair?
She seems to be from off the island. How do I say it, she has a really nice figure...
...Perhaps you mean that woman from earlier. I don't know her...
Alain said she was a friend of his, but... I would never associate with someone who would open a coffin.
Eh...? W-Why would she open the coffin...?
How should I know! What an absurd woman! Good grief...
R-Right...
(That's everyone I wanted to talk to... Let's organize this information.)
(Most of the attendees knew about the "Shadow Sickness". But they all unanimously called it a "superstition" or a "ghost story".)
(None of them think that monsters called "Shadows" actually exist...)
(What's interesting are the answers from Karikiri-san, the chief priest, and Hishigata-sensei.)
(If what they said is true, "Shadows" already existed on this island since at least the Edo period, and were frequently sighted up until before the war...)
(And the "old medical records" left at Hishigata Clinic... It seems worth investigating.)
(And more than anything... Surprisingly, many people knew about the "woman with glasses"!!)
(A local named "Hizuru Minakata"... That must be Nagumo-sensei's real name!)
("Ryunosuke Nagumo" is a pseudonymous author, so no one knows that Nagumo-sensei = <color=#GF_TIPS_033>Hizuru Minakata</color>!)
(And she used to be a regular at Kofune... What a near miss we had!!)
